title: stenostephanus cuscoensis
intro: Stenostephanus cuscoensis, also known as Yellow Bird's Tongue, is a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, native to the Andean regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenoids and flavonoids. The health benefits of Stenostephanus cuscoensis are believed to include the reduction of inflammation, improved wound healing, and the treatment of respiratory infections. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made from the leaves and flowers, which can be used to make teas, infusions, or dried extracts.
